page: Amazon Web Services c6id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c6id.16xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by its top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests and PassMark CPU scores. The processor's support for AVX-512 and advanced cryptography instructions drives strong performance in encryption, software compilation, and ray tracing. While single-core performance and memory bandwidth sit in the average tier, multi-threaded decompression and static web serving throughput are highly competitive.

The instance offers a high-density compute profile with integrated high-capacity local NVMe storage, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring both high CPU performance and low-latency local disk access. However, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications, which may exhaust the 128 GB memory pool before utilizing the full capacity of the 64 vCPUs.

This hardware configuration is suited for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from local scratch space, such as high-performance web serving, database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale language model text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 128 GB of DDR4 memory, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network speed of 25 Gbps. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, particularly in software compilation, cryptography, and database operations. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a dense compute profile, though it presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. The integrated high-capacity NVMe storage enhances cost efficiency for workloads requiring rapid local read/write capabilities. This instance is well-suited for high-performance web serving, distributed database nodes, and multi-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for database and compilation workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized platform combining sixty-four high-frequency vCPUs with high-capacity local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.

128 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU, cryptography, and software compilation performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Suited for database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded workloads
